summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that sjcl uses weak PBKDF2 iteration counts (10000 default, 1000 fallback) which are far below OWASP recommendations of 600,000+, enabling rapid brute-force attacks against password-protected data.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should demonstrate that sjcl uses iteration counts 60-600x lower than recommended, enabling rapid password cracking attacks.
actual_observed_result: The verification script confirmed the weak PBKDF2 iteration counts in both the defaults (10000) and the cachedPbkdf2 fallback (1000). Testing showed that encryption uses these weak defaults, making passwords 60-600x faster to crack than with recommended iteration counts.
verification_method: The verification script analyzes the source code to identify iteration count defaults and demonstrates encryption using weak parameters. The proof signal is the presence of iteration counts far below security recommendations.
evidence_produced_by_run: Source code at core/convenience.js:14 shows iter:10000 default
Source code at core/convenience.js:316 shows 1000 iteration fallback
Encryption operations use weak defaults automatically
Iteration counts are 60-600x lower than OWASP recommendations

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh uses the existing target repository checkout at /Users/mac/ResearchSpace/opencode/EvoHunt-DryRun/advisory_67122281/repo and mounts it into the container.
